title: Artificial Intelligence Fundamentals & Terminology
focus: Core concepts, stages of AI, Machine Learning types, and LLM architectures.
rationale: Foundational AI terminology is frequently tested in UPSC Prelims Science & Tech section, especially distinguishing between types of machine learning and AI stages.
sectionExcerpt: Covers the stages of AI (ANI, AGI, ASI), Machine Learning types (Supervised, Unsupervised), NLP, Neural Networks, Deep Learning, LLMs, GPT architecture, and Agentic AI like India's Kruti.
evidenceBullets: ANI specializes in one area; AGI learns continuously like humans; ASI surpasses human cognitive abilities.
Supervised learning uses labeled datasets; Unsupervised uses unlabeled datasets.
GPT stands for Generative Pre-trained Transformer, built on deep learning architecture.
Agentic AI systems autonomously make decisions and adapt (e.g., India's first agentic AI Kruti).

title: Bharat Gen and AI Ecosystem Initiatives
focus: India's indigenous AI initiatives, IndiaAI Mission, and global AI pacts.
rationale: Government missions, implementing agencies, and international declarations are prime targets for UPSC questions. Bharat Gen's implementation details and the Bletchley Declaration are highly relevant.
sectionExcerpt: Details the launch of Bharat Gen, an indigenous multimodal LLM for Indian languages under NM-ICPS, implemented by IIT Bombay. Also covers the IndiaAI Mission, AIRAWAT, GPAI, Europe's AI Act, and the Bletchley Declaration.
evidenceBullets: Bharat Gen is India's first indigenously developed AI-based Multimodal LLM for all 22 scheduled Indian languages.
Bharat Gen is developed under NM-ICPS and implemented through TIH at IIT Bombay with DST funding.
IndiaAI Mission is implemented by the IndiaAI IBD under Digital India Corporation (MeitY).
AIRAWAT provides a common compute platform for AI research.
Bletchley Declaration (2023) establishes a shared understanding of frontier AI risks, signed by 28 countries and the EU.

title: Deepfakes
focus: Definition, underlying technology, and recent legislative actions regarding Deepfakes.
rationale: Deepfakes are a major contemporary issue. The underlying technology (deep learning) and recent legislative actions form a distinct, testable micro-topic.
sectionExcerpt: Defines Deepfakes as AI-manipulated video, photo, or audio recordings using deep learning techniques, and mentions the US Take It Down Act enacted to tackle harmful deepfakes online.
evidenceBullets: Deepfakes use deep learning techniques to manipulate video, photo, or audio recordings.
The US recently enacted the Take It Down Act to tackle harmful deepfakes online.
